title: Replace Tabs with Button Navigation in Slide-Out Panels
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want slide-out panels to use button-style navigation instead of tabs, so that the UX clearly indicates mutually exclusive selections rather than co-existing views.
description:
Replace tab-style navigation in metrics and migration slide-out panels with Bootstrap button navigation (nav-tabs JS behavior). This creates a UX distinction: tabs group co-existing elements, while button navigation indicates mutually exclusive selections.

## Scope
- Metrics page slide-out panel
- Migration (object detail) page slide-out panel
- Use Bootstrap 5 nav-tabs JavaScript behavior for toggle functionality
acceptance_criteria: Metrics slide-out uses button-style navigation instead of tabs
Migration slide-out uses button-style navigation instead of tabs
Navigation style clearly indicates mutually exclusive selection
Toggle functionality works correctly between sections
ux_changes: Slide-out panels on metrics and object detail pages updated from tabs to button-style navigation for clearer UX semantics (mutually exclusive vs co-existing selections).
implementation_notes: Ryan explained the UX distinction: tabs for grouping co-existing elements, button navigation for mutually exclusive selections. This is a subtle but important consistency principle across the app.
comment_summary: Developer initially confused about whether to update the sidebar tabs or the top navigation. Ryan clarified the UX distinction between tabs (co-existing groups) and button navigation (mutually exclusive selections). Developer updated both metrics and object detail slide-outs.
